jobs.description: Data Scientist (all genders) - on-site presence - Swatch Group Data Scientist (all genders) - on-site presence The company Behind every record, there are years of intensive training and the athlete’s desire to produce excellent performances. Behind the result, there is profound know-how and the high precision of Swiss Timing’s state-of-the-art technology. If you want to participate in the future development of high level sport events ... we are looking for you! Globally active, our company is constantly seeking for employees determined to progress and strive in a highly innovative environment. As a company of the Swatch Group, we are committed to quality of life, health and safety, and the environment. We are eager to hire employees with a sustainable mindset. Job description Data Scientist in Swiss Timing will play a key role in transforming raw sports data into meaningful insights that support performance analysis, data-driven decision making, and data storytelling. Your primary mission is the refinement, interpretation, and advanced analysis of measured sports data, contributing to both live and post-processing systems used across a wide range of sports and technologies. You will work with signal processing, statistical analysis, and machine learning, developing robust algorithms and analytics pipelines that convert sensor data into reliable, sport-specific performance metrics. This role requires strong scientific thinking, hands-on algorithm development, and close collaboration with interdisciplinary teams. Design, implement, and maintain data processing pipelines and algorithms for live and post-event analytics across firmware and software stages Apply signal processing techniques (e.g. filtering, smoothing, resampling) and implement position estimation using Real Time Tracking System, computer vision, and IMU data Analyze and contextualize sports data; develop activity recognition algorithms and compute key performance metrics (e.g. speed, acceleration, jump height, rotation) Extract and communicate actionable insights for athletes, federations, and media Apply statistical analysis to complex datasets and develop, evaluate, and improve machine learning models (supervised & unsupervised) Create tailored analyses and visualizations for internal and external stakeholders Ensure data quality through systematic testing, validation, and performance monitoring; enhance robustness and reliability of outputs Contribute to innovation by developing new systems, analytics pipelines, and continuously improving existing technologies Profile Strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills High affinity for data-driven problem solving and complex systems Structured, independent, and solution-oriented working style Ability to communicate complex data and insights in a clear and understandable way Interest in sports and data-driven performance analysis Detail-oriented and quality-focused, especially when working with sensitive data Team player with the ability to collaborate effectively in interdisciplinary environments Curiosity and motivation to continuously learn and explore new technologies and methods Proactive mindset with a drive to improve and innovate existing solutions Professional requirements Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or a related field Strong background in statistics, machine learning, and signal processing Proficiency in Python; experience with C++ or C# is an advantage Experience with data visualization and scientific analysis workflows Solid understanding of scientific methodology and experimental validation Ability to work independently on complex analytical problems while collaborating effectively in a team Languages Good communication skills in English are required. Contact Benjamin Adler HR Business Partner Job location Rue de l'Envers 1 2606 Corgémont (Bern) Switzerland Company address Swiss Timing LTD Rue de l’Envers 1 P.O. box 138 CH-2606 Corgémont Apply for this job Share this job offer

descriptionByLocale.en:
## Descrizione
Data Scientist (all genders) - on-site presence - Swatch Group Data Scientist (all genders) - on-site presence The company Behind every record, there are years of intensive training and the athlete’s desire to produce excellent performances. Behind the result, there is profound know-how and the high precision of Swiss Timing’s state-of-the-art technology. If you want to participate in the future development of high level sport events ... we are looking for you! Globally active, our company is constantly seeking for employees determined to progress and strive in a highly innovative environment. As a company of the Swatch Group, we are committed to quality of life, health and safety, and the environment. We are eager to hire employees with a sustainable mindset.

## Mansioni
- Your primary mission is the refinement, interpretation, and advanced analysis of measured sports data, contributing to both live and post-processing systems used across a wide range of sports and technologies.
- You will work with signal processing, statistical analysis, and machine learning, developing robust algorithms and analytics pipelines that convert sensor data into reliable, sport-specific performance metrics.
- Design, implement, and maintain data processing pipelines and algorithms for live and post-event analytics across firmware and software stages
- Apply signal processing techniques (e.g. filtering, smoothing, resampling) and implement position estimation using Real Time Tracking System, computer vision, and IMU data
- Analyze and contextualize sports data; develop activity recognition algorithms and compute key performance metrics (e.g. speed, acceleration, jump height, rotation)
- Extract and communicate actionable insights for athletes, federations, and media
- Apply statistical analysis to complex datasets and develop, evaluate, and improve machine learning models (supervised & unsupervised)
- Create tailored analyses and visualizations for internal and external stakeholders
- Ensure data quality through systematic testing, validation, and performance monitoring; enhance robustness and reliability of outputs
- Contribute to innovation by developing new systems, analytics pipelines, and continuously improving existing technologies

## Requisiti
### Profilo
- Strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills
- High affinity for data-driven problem solving and complex systems
- Structured, independent, and solution-oriented working style
- Ability to communicate complex data and insights in a clear and understandable way
- Interest in sports and data-driven performance analysis
- Detail-oriented and quality-focused, especially when working with sensitive data
- Team player with the ability to collaborate effectively in interdisciplinary environments
- Curiosity and motivation to continuously learn and explore new technologies and methods
- Proactive mindset with a drive to improve and innovate existing solutions

### Requisiti professionali
- Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or a related field
- Strong background in statistics, machine learning, and signal processing
- Proficiency in Python; experience with C++ or C# is an advantage
- Experience with data visualization and scientific analysis workflows
- Solid understanding of scientific methodology and experimental validation
- Ability to work independently on complex analytical problems while collaborating effectively in a team

### Lingue
- Good communication skills in English are required.

## Contatto
- Contact Benjamin Adler HR Business Partner
- Job location Rue de l'Envers 1 2606 Corgémont (Bern) Switzerland
- Company address Swiss Timing LTD Rue de l’Envers 1 P.O. box 138 CH-2606 Corgémont
- Apply for this job
- Share this job offer
descriptionByLocale.it:
## Descrizione
Data Scientist (tutti i sessi) - presenza in loco - Swatch Group Data Scientist (tutti i generi) - presenza in loco L'azienda Dietro ogni record, ci sono anni di formazione intensiva e il desiderio dell'atleta di produrre prestazioni eccellenti. Dietro il risultato, c’è un profondo know-how e l’alta precisione della tecnologia all’avanguardia di Swiss Timing. Se vuoi partecipare al futuro sviluppo di eventi sportivi di alto livello... ti stiamo cercando! Globalmente attiva, la nostra azienda è costantemente alla ricerca di dipendenti determinati a progredire e a sforzarsi in un ambiente altamente innovativo. In qualità di azienda del Gruppo Swatch, siamo impegnati nella qualità della vita, della salute e della sicurezza e dell'ambiente. Siamo desiderosi di assumere dipendenti con una mentalità sostenibile.

## Mansioni
- La vostra missione primaria è la raffinatezza, l'interpretazione e l'analisi avanzata dei dati sportivi misurati, contribuendo sia ai sistemi live che post-processing utilizzati in una vasta gamma di sport e tecnologie.
- Si lavorerà con l'elaborazione del segnale, l'analisi statistica e l'apprendimento automatico, lo sviluppo di algoritmi robusti e condotte di analisi che convertono i dati dei sensori in metriche di prestazioni specifiche per lo sport.
- Progettare, implementare e mantenere pipeline di elaborazione dati e algoritmi per analisi dal vivo e post-evento attraverso le fasi del firmware e del software
- Applicare tecniche di elaborazione del segnale (ad esempio filtraggio, levigatura, ricampionamento) e implementare la stima della posizione utilizzando il sistema di monitoraggio del tempo reale, la visione del computer e i dati IMU
- Analizzare e contestualizzare i dati sportivi; sviluppare algoritmi di riconoscimento delle attività e calcolare le metriche delle prestazioni chiave (ad esempio velocità, accelerazione, altezza di salto, rotazione)
- Estrarre e comunicare intuizioni attuabili per atleti, federazioni e media
- Applicare analisi statistiche su set di dati complessi e sviluppare, valutare e migliorare i modelli di apprendimento automatico (superviso e non supervisionato)
- Creare analisi e visualizzazioni su misura per gli stakeholder interni ed esterni
- Garantire la qualità dei dati attraverso test sistematici, validazione e monitoraggio delle prestazioni; migliorare robustezza e affidabilità delle uscite
- Contribuire all'innovazione sviluppando nuovi sistemi, pipeline di analisi e migliorando continuamente le tecnologie esistenti

# Requisiti #
#### Profilo
- Forte capacità di pensiero analitico e concettuale
- Alta affinità per la risoluzione dei problemi data-driven e sistemi complessi
- Stile di lavoro strutturato, indipendente e orientato alla soluzione
- Capacità di comunicare dati e approfondimenti complessi in modo chiaro e comprensibile
- Interessi nell'analisi delle prestazioni dello sport e dei dati
- orientato al dettaglio e focalizzato sulla qualità, soprattutto quando si lavora con dati sensibili
- Team player con la capacità di collaborare efficacemente in ambienti interdisciplinari
- Curiosità e motivazione per imparare ed esplorare continuamente nuove tecnologie e metodi
- Mindset proattivo con una spinta per migliorare e innovare le soluzioni esistenti

### Requisiti professionali
- Laurea in Scienze dei dati, Informatica, Ingegneria, Fisica, Matematica, o un campo relativo
- Sfondo forte nelle statistiche, nell'apprendimento automatico e nell'elaborazione dei segnali
- Proficienza in Python; l'esperienza con C++ o C# è un vantaggio
- Esperienza con la visualizzazione dei dati e i flussi di analisi scientifica
- Solida comprensione della metodologia scientifica e validazione sperimentale
- Capacità di lavorare in modo indipendente su complessi problemi analitici collaborando efficacemente in un team

### Lingue
- Sono necessarie buone capacità di comunicazione in inglese.

## Contatto
- Contatta Benjamin Adler HR Business Partner
- Luogo di lavoro Rue de l'Envers 1 2606 Corgémont (Bern) Svizzera
- Indirizzo dell'azienda Swiss Timing LTD Rue de l'Envers 1 P.O. box 138 CH-2606 Corgémont
- Applicare per questo lavoro
- Condividi questa offerta di lavoro
descriptionByLocale.de: Beschreibung Data Scientist (alle Geschlechter) - Präsenz vor Ort - Swatch Group Data Scientist (alle Geschlechter) - Präsenz vor Ort Hinter jeder Platte gibt es jahrelang intensives Training und der Wunsch des Athleten, hervorragende Leistungen zu produzieren. Hinter dem Ergebnis steckt tiefes Know-how und die hohe Präzision der hochmodernen Technologie von Swiss Timing. Wenn Sie an der zukünftigen Entwicklung von High-Level-Sport-Events teilnehmen wollen... suchen wir Sie! Weltweit aktiv ist unser Unternehmen ständig auf der Suche nach Mitarbeitern, die entschlossen sind, in einem hochinnovativen Umfeld voranzuschreiten und zu streben. Als Unternehmen der Swatch Group setzen wir uns für Lebensqualität, Gesundheit und Sicherheit und Umwelt ein. Wir sind gespannt, Mitarbeiter mit einer nachhaltigen Einstellung zu beschäftigen. Â Mansioni - Ihre primäre Mission ist die Verfeinerung, Interpretation und fortschrittliche Analyse von gemessenen Sportdaten und trägt zu sowohl lebenden als auch nachverarbeitenden Systemen bei, die über ein breites Spektrum an Sport- und Technologien genutzt werden. - Ja. Sie arbeiten mit Signalverarbeitung, statistischer Analyse und maschinellem Lernen, entwickeln robuste Algorithmen und Analytik-Pipelines, die Sensordaten in zuverlässige, sportspezifische Leistungsmetriken umwandeln. - Erstellung, Implementierung und Aufrechterhaltung der Datenverarbeitung Pipelines und Algorithmen für Live- und Post-Event-Analysen über Firmware und Software-Stufen - Anwendung von Signalverarbeitungstechniken (z.B. Filtern, Glätten, Resampling) und Umsetzung von Positionsschätzungen mittels Real Time Tracking System, Computer Vision und IMU-Daten - Analyse und Kontextanalyse von Sportdaten Kontakt Benjamin Adler HR Business Partner - Job Location Rue de l'Envers 1 2606 Corgémont (Bern) Schweiz - Firmenadresse Swiss Timing LTD Rue de l'Envers 1 P.O. Box 138 CH-2606 Corgémont - Bewerben Sie sich für diesen Job - Teilen Sie diesen Jobangebot
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Data Scientist (tous les sexes) - présence sur place - Swatch Group Data Scientist (tous les sexes) - présence sur place La compagnie Derrière chaque record, il y a des années d'entraînement intensif et les athlètes veulent produire d'excellentes performances. Derrière le résultat, il y a un savoir-faire profond et la haute précision de la technologie de pointe du Timing suisse. Si vous voulez participer au développement futur d'événements sportifs de haut niveau ... nous sommes à votre recherche! Globalement actif, notre entreprise cherche constamment des employés déterminés à progresser et à travailler dans un environnement hautement innovant. En tant qu'entreprise du Swatch Group, nous sommes engagés dans la qualité de vie, la santé et la sécurité, et l'environnement. Nous sommes impatients d'embaucher des employés avec un état d'esprit durable. ## Mansioni - Votre mission principale est le raffinement, l'interprétation et l'analyse avancée des données sportives mesurées, contribuant aux systèmes de traitement en direct et post-traitement utilisés dans un large éventail de sports et de technologies. - Oui.
